pr.title: Added fox item attr and some small fixes
pr.author: AaronB-Hub

summary: Closed, unmerged PR that attempted to split several item runtime vars from article specialAttributes, especially for Fox laser/blaster-related item attributes, and cleaned up incorrect union member usage in multiple common item files. The author stated the PR failed and was closed to resolve issues before opening a new one, so the changes should be treated as useful evidence of an attempted cleanup rather than accepted project state.
what_changed: Added explicit FoxBlasterAttr, FoxLaserAttr, and FoxIllusionAttr structs in itCharItems.h while renaming FoxLaserVars fields from offset-style names xDD4/xDD8/xDDC/xDE0 to semantic runtime fields scale, angle, speed, and pos.

Changed itfoxlaser.c to use FoxLaserAttr from item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes for article constants such as lifetime and scale, while keeping per-instance FoxLaserVars in item->xDD4_itemVar.foxlaser.
Renamed the it_8029C504 final parameter from arg9 to speed in itfoxlaser.c and itfoxlaser.h, matching its use in velocity calculations.

Renamed ItCapsuleAttrs to ItCapsuleVars for xDD4_itemVar storage and introduced a separate ItCapsuleAttr for x4_specialAttributes, then updated itcapsule.c to use attr->x4 for HSD_Randi and attr->x0 for it_8026F3D4.
Added or reorganized common item attribute structs including HeartContainerAttr, ItLGunRayAttr, ItLGunBeamAttr, FFlowerAttr, and ItLGunAttr comments with observed table values in brackets.

Fixed several apparent incorrect item union member accesses: egg code now uses xDD4_itemVar.egg.x0 instead of capsule.x0; fire flower uses xDD4_itemVar.fflower; Maxim Tomato writes xDD4_itemVar.MaximTomato.heal_amount instead of capsule.x0; Heart Container uses xDD4_itemVar.HeartContainer fields.
Updated itheart.c to read HeartContainerAttr fields from specialAttributes and map them to velocity, heal amount, flags, and rotation values.
Updated itlgunbeam.c to use ItLGunBeamAttr.lifetime instead of generic x0.
Updated src/melee/it/types.h so the Item xDD4_itemVar union uses ItCapsuleVars capsule instead of ItCapsuleAttrs capsule.
smart_moves: Separated article-level specialAttributes from per-item xDD4 runtime variables, reducing the risk of treating immutable item data as live state.
Used semantic field names where evidence was strong: FoxLaserVars.scale, angle, speed, pos; FoxLaserAttr.lifetime and scale; ItLGunBeamAttr.lifetime; MaximTomato.heal_amount.
Preserved offset comments such as ip+DD4/ip+DD8 while renaming fields, which helps decompilers maintain layout confidence while improving readability.

Added bracketed constant comments from item attribute data, for example FoxLaserAttr lifetime [35], scale [3], ItLGunRayAttr speed [5], lifetime [80], and HeartContainerAttr heal [100], making future struct identification easier.
Corrected union member selection to item-specific names rather than reusing capsule storage names for unrelated items, making the xDD4_itemVar union more self-documenting.
decomp_lessons: For item structs, distinguish xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes attribute structs from xDD4_itemVar runtime structs even when both have similar field layouts.
Offset-based names like xDD4, xDD8, xDDC are useful during initial matching, but can be safely upgraded to semantic names when usage proves meaning across multiple functions.
When a union is shared by many item kinds, incorrect but layout-compatible member access may still compile and match; later cleanup should switch to the item-specific union member to document intent.
Keep layout comments when renaming fields in decomp headers so future match work can reconcile semantic names with original item memory offsets.

Attribute table constants are valuable evidence for naming fields such as lifetime, max_scale, heal amount, y velocity, and rotation step, but uncertain fields should remain xN with comments rather than overnamed.
naming_conventions: Use Attr suffix for article specialAttributes structs, for example FoxLaserAttr, HeartContainerAttr, ItCapsuleAttr, FFlowerAttr.
Use Vars suffix for per-instance item variable structs stored under Item.xDD4_itemVar, for example FoxLaserVars, ItCapsuleVars, ItLGunRayVars.
Prefer semantic runtime field names when behavior is clear: scale, angle, speed, pos, timer, lifetime, max_scale, heal_amount.
Retain xN field names for unknown attribute fields and annotate them with observed constants or suspected purpose rather than inventing names.
Preserve offset comments using ip+DD4 style for item variable structs to document union placement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed fields without changing apparent struct layout, using offsets and types to preserve ABI/matching behavior.
Mapped specialAttributes loads to typed attr pointers so fields like lifetime and scale could replace raw x0/xDD4-style access.
Used item behavior to infer field names: Fox laser speed feeds cosf/sinf velocity, angle feeds atan2/normalization, scale feeds HSD_JObjSetScaleZ, and lifetime feeds it_80275158.
Identified layout-compatible union misuse by comparing item-specific code paths and changed member names while preserving the same underlying xDD4 storage.
review_feedback: No review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.

The PR body says: "When the PR failed, I closed it to resolve the issues and then open a new one. Hopefully this is less work on y'all, but lmk if that's annoying." This is evidence the PR was intentionally closed after failure and not merged.
searchable_terms: Added fox item attr and some small fixes
follow_up_queries: Find the replacement PR opened after PR #1459 to see which parts were accepted.
Search for FoxLaserAttr and FoxLaserVars in later commits to verify final field names and layout.
Compare item xDD4_itemVar union member names after this PR to confirm egg, fflower, HeartContainer, and MaximTomato cleanup landed elsewhere.
Check whether HeartContainerAttr bitfield layout and x4.flags typing survived review in a merged PR.
Search for ItCapsuleAttrs versus ItCapsuleVars to determine final naming convention for capsule runtime vars.
